title: phyllanthus arboreus
intro: Phyllanthus arboreus, also known as the Malayan gooseberry or the Singapore Rhubarb, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating liver and kidney disorders, and acting as an antidote for poison. Phyllanthus arboreus can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as poultices and infusions.
